name: Release Provenance Control Baseline
summary: The release provenance control has a source-backed baseline: SLSA Build Provenance v1.2 defines the kinds of build-production evidence a provenance record should preserve, while leaving release-specific assurance to later project evidence.
main_blockers: No project-specific release, builder, or attestation has been reviewed in this baseline.
best_current_signals: The SLSA Build Provenance v1.2 specification describes a predicate for how artifacts were produced.
The specification includes build definition, run details, builder identity, and dependency-related fields relevant to release provenance review.
interpretive_note: This baseline establishes the control model for release provenance review. It should not be read as evidence that any specific software release satisfies that model.
limitations: This claim is bounded to the SLSA Build Provenance v1.2 specification.
It does not verify a release artifact, build platform, repository, dependency set, or signed attestation.
Operational assurance requires a later bundle with release-specific source material.
update_notes: Use a review update or a release-specific baseline review bundle when reviewing a concrete project release.

supporting_evidence.label: SLSA provenance control model
supporting_evidence.conclusion: SLSA Build Provenance v1.2 is an appropriate source-backed baseline for what release provenance evidence should describe.
supporting_evidence.rationale: The extracted finding identifies the provenance predicate and fields used to describe how artifacts were produced and the builder context.
limitations: The support is specification-level, not project-specific.
It does not prove that any given release has complete or trustworthy provenance.
